meaning: should; it is important to
example_ja: 毎日復習することだ。
example_en: You should review every day.

meaning: there is no need to; never happens
example_ja: 心配することはない。
example_en: There is no need to worry.

meaning: pretend to; act as if
example_ja: 知らないそうにしていた。
example_en: I pretended not to know.

meaning: time; place; situation; about to
example_ja: 今、出かけるところです。
example_en: I am about to go out now.

meaning: about; concerning; regarding
example_ja: 環境について話す。
example_en: Talk about the environment.

meaning: by means of; due to; depending on; passive agent
example_ja: この本は有名な作家によって書かれた。
example_en: This book was written by a famous author.

meaning: according to
example_ja: 天気予報によると、明日は雨だ。
example_en: According to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow.

meaning: toward; in contrast to; regarding
example_ja: お客様に対して丁寧に話す。
example_en: Speak politely toward customers.

meaning: only; just; full of; tendency to
example_ja: 毎日ゲームをしているばかりだ。
example_en: I do nothing but play games every day.

meaning: not only ... but also
formation: Verb-plain / i-adjective / na-adjective + ばかりでなく
example_ja: 彼女は頭がいいばかりでなく、優しい。
example_en: She is not only smart but also kind.

meaning: it is natural/normal to; one should
example_ja: 子供のころはよく遊んだものだ。
example_en: I used to play a lot as a child.

meaning: should not; it's not right to
example_ja: 人の悪口を言うものではない。
example_en: You shouldn't speak ill of others.

meaning: way/manner of; like; in order to
example_ja: 泳ぎ方を教えてください。
example_en: Please teach me how to swim.

meaning: make sure to; try to
example_ja: 毎日日本語を話すようにしている。
example_en: I make sure to speak Japanese every day.

meaning: come to be able to; reach the point where
example_ja: 毎日練習したら、泳げるようになった。
example_en: After practicing every day, I became able to swim.

meaning: seems; I heard; typical (less casual than みたい)
example_ja: 彼は怒っているらしい。
example_en: He seems angry.

meaning: there is no way that; impossible
formation: Verb-plain / i-adjective / na-adjective + わけがない
example_ja: あの人が嘘をつくわけがない。
example_en: There is no way that person would lie.

meaning: it is natural that; no wonder; as expected
example_ja: 毎日勉強しているから、上手になるわけだ。
example_en: Since you study every day, it's natural that you improve.

meaning: it doesn't mean that; not necessarily
formation: Verb-plain / i-adjective / na-adjective + わけではない
example_ja: 嫌いなわけではない。
example_en: It's not that I dislike it.

meaning: on the one hand; on the other hand; while
example_ja: この町は静かな一方、不便だ。
example_en: This town is quiet, but on the other hand, it's inconvenient.
